notes:
Batch E (67 entries) — shared_session network errors + workspace menus + warpify settings + block_list_element + notebook.

File counts:
- network.rs: 16 — all user-facing error/status strings from *_string helpers and FailedToJoinReason::user_facing_error_message. Translated; full-width punctuation, 您 register, brand Warp/Agent kept English.
- workspace/mod.rs: 15 — menu labels / EditableBinding descriptions / MAC_MENUS_CONTEXT names. All UI. 'Configure Keyboard Shortcuts...' / 'Configure Warpify...' → trailing '……'.
- warpify/settings.rs: 16 — 4 doc-comment macro false positives (lines 166/167/169/177 inside lazy_static!), 2 .expect panic messages (lines 174/180), 8 setting descriptions (schema/UI tooltip, translated), 2 enum display_name UI labels.
- block_list_element.rs: 13 — 11 .expect/expect-message panic strings; 1 telemetry_payload (line 3572 debug-only memory stats overlay text gated on DebugSettings::should_show_memory_stats); 2 UI banner strings ('Conversation restored', 'Previous session').
- notebook.rs: 12 — 1 .expect panic (line 155); 2 .context() args fed into report_if_error! → sentry telemetry; 4 safe_info! log macro args → log::info!; 4 user-facing UI strings (toast errors for secrets, deletion notices, trash notice).

Uncertainties:
- warpify settings descriptions are exposed via schema description fields. They appear to back the settings UI tooltips / JSON schema docs — treated as UI per prior batch convention. If the reviewer determines these are only consumed by JSON schema docs (developer-facing), they could be re-flagged.
- block_list_element.rs line 3572 (memory stats overlay) sits in a real on-screen text element but is gated behind DebugSettings::should_show_memory_stats(). Flagged telemetry_payload because it's developer diagnostics, never seen by end users. Reasonable alternative would be to translate as UI; chose flag to keep the format-string fragile placeholders safe.
- 'Agent conversation list view' (workspace line 817): kept brand 'Agent' English per glossary; chose '对话列表视图' for 'conversation list view'.
- 'Send feedback with Oz': kept Oz English per glossary.
- 'Configure Warpify...' / 'warpify' / 'warpify prompt' in settings descriptions: 'Warpify' is a Warp feature name — kept English, lower-case 'warpify' in descriptions also kept English as a verb/feature reference.
- 'Toggle Code Review': kept 'Code Review' English per glossary.
- No search_terms entries appeared in this batch.
